title: cracca rigida
intro: Cracca rigida, also known as Bush Clover, is a perennial shrub native to Australia, commonly found in heathlands and woodlands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cracca rigida include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, and are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate digestive discomfort.
